persona.summary: You are the welcoming face of beginner gym culture, blending powerlifting with approachable high-protein cooking.
content_pillars: high-protein meals on a student budget
persona.audience: college students who want to start lifting but feel out of place at the gym
persona.posting_cadence: 4 posts/week, heavier on Sunday meal-prep days

input.events: midterms in week 2; spring break road trip in week 4

items.idea: My $20 meal prep for a week of high-protein lunches. Show the budget grocery haul, the quick prep, and the final stacked containers.
items.filming_suggestion: Use top-down shots for cooking sequences. Film near a window for bright, natural light. Add text overlay with macros per meal.

items.idea: POV: You think everyone is staring at you in the weight room. A relatable clip followed by a direct-to-camera reassurance that they're not.
items.filming_suggestion: Film the POV part discreetly at the gym. For the pep talk, sit in your room and talk to the camera like you're on FaceTime with a friend.

items.idea: The not-scary way to set up for a barbell squat. Break down 3 simple steps: bar height, hand placement, and walking it out.
items.filming_suggestion: Set your phone on a tripod at a 45-degree angle to the squat rack to clearly show your body positioning from the side.

items.idea: The laziest 30g protein snack ever: Greek yogurt protein bark. A quick, goofy tutorial showing how easy it is.
items.filming_suggestion: Film in your kitchen with fast cuts. End with a satisfying ASMR crunch sound as you take a bite.

items.idea: Let's talk about progressive overload. Explain the concept in simple terms, using plates on a barbell as a visual aid.
items.filming_suggestion: In the gym, stack plates onto a bar as you talk via voiceover, visually demonstrating the 'progressive' part.

items.idea: What's in my gym bag to feel 100% prepared and less anxious. Showcase essentials like headphones, a water bottle, and a workout plan.
items.filming_suggestion: A flat-lay shot of the items, then quick cuts of you packing each one into your bag. Use a trending 'pack with me' sound.

items.idea: Common mistake: Where to put your hands for a bench press? Show a narrow vs. a good-width grip and explain the difference.
items.filming_suggestion: Film from an angle above the bench looking down towards your chest to clearly show hand spacing on the bar.

items.idea: Midterms week meal prep: Brain fuel edition. Focus on quick, no-reheat meals you can eat while studying.
items.filming_suggestion: Show a textbook and highlighters on the counter next to your meal prep containers to connect with the student audience.

items.idea: How I use a 30-min workout to manage study stress, not add to it. A short, gentle routine for mental clarity.
items.filming_suggestion: Use a calming voiceover and show clips of simple exercises. The mood should be relaxing, not intense.

items.idea: My 3-minute protein coffee recipe for fueling a late-night study session. A quick tutorial with instant coffee and protein shake.
items.filming_suggestion: Film in your kitchen with moody, lower lighting to fit the 'late-night' vibe. A satisfying swirl shot is key.

items.idea: One simple cue that finally fixed my deadlift form. Show a 'before' (rounded back) and 'after' (flat back) using the cue.
items.filming_suggestion: A clear side-profile shot is essential here. Use text overlay to pop the cue on screen during the 'after' clip.

items.idea: Friendly reminder: It's okay if your workout is just 20 minutes of stretching during midterms week. Rest is productive.
items.filming_suggestion: Film yourself doing some simple stretches on a yoga mat in your room. Keep the tone gentle and reassuring.

items.idea: We survived midterms! Let's make celebratory (but still healthy) high-protein pancakes.
items.filming_suggestion: Bright, cheerful lighting. Get a slow-motion shot of pouring the syrup for a satisfying payoff.

items.idea: Rating my favorite 'main character' songs for lifting. A fun, lip-sync video showing you getting hyped in your headphones.
items.filming_suggestion: In the gym before a lift. Use quick cuts between you pointing to text on screen listing the songs, and you mouthing the lyrics.

items.idea: Come grocery shopping with me: My top 5 high-protein finds at Trader Joe's. A saveable guide for students.
items.filming_suggestion: Film POV-style with your phone in the grocery cart. Point to the items on the shelf and use text overlay for price and protein content.

items.idea: Let's master the hip-hinge at home. A tutorial using a wall or a broomstick to learn the pattern without weights.
items.filming_suggestion: Film against a plain wall in your house so the movement is clear. A side profile view is best.

items.idea: My gym outfit rotation for when I want to feel confident, not self-conscious. Focus on comfort and function.
items.filming_suggestion: A try-on style video in your bedroom. Use a trending outfit transition sound to reveal each look.

items.idea: What I eat in a day as a student who actually lifts. Quick, simple, high-protein meals shown in a montage.
items.filming_suggestion: Compile short clips of each meal throughout the day. Start with making your first meal in the morning and end with your last.

items.idea: Are you making this common lat pulldown mistake? Show the wrong way (shrugging shoulders) vs. the right way (depressing scapula).
items.filming_suggestion: Film from a front/side angle to clearly show the shoulder and back movement. Use a pointer emoji in your text to highlight the area.

items.idea: Tried the assisted pull-up machine for the first time — getting braver. Show the nerves, the shaky reps, and the proud smile.
items.filming_suggestion: Set up a tripod and film the whole set, including the moment before you start and your reaction after. Focus on the effort, not perfection.

items.idea: My secret to making protein oatmeal that doesn't taste like cardboard. A quick tip about mixing and toppings.
items.filming_suggestion: A top-down shot into the bowl. Show the creamy texture and add colorful toppings for a visually appealing final shot.

items.idea: Packing high-protein snacks for our spring break road trip. Save money and stay on track!
items.filming_suggestion: A 'pack with me' style video. Lay out all the snacks on your floor or bed first for a satisfying shot, then pack them.

items.idea: Friendly reminder: Your muscles grow when you REST. Take the spring break guilt-free.
items.filming_suggestion: A simple selfie-style video talking to the camera from the passenger seat of the car. Authentic and low-production is the vibe.

items.idea: No gym on vacation? No problem. A 5-minute bodyweight squat routine you can do in your hotel room.
items.filming_suggestion: Find a spot with good natural light from a window in your room. Prop your phone up on a desk or chair.

items.idea: How to build a high-protein meal at a gas station. A real-world example of picking smart choices on the road.
items.filming_suggestion: Film POV-style walking through the gas station. Show the items you pick (jerky, protein shake, nuts) and the final combo.

items.idea: If you needed to hear this today: It's okay to fall off your routine on vacation. The gym will be there when you get back.
items.filming_suggestion: Film against a scenic spring break backdrop, like a beach or a viewpoint. Connect the idea of a mental break with the physical one.

items.idea: A non-fitness 'core memory' from spring break. A montage of happy, goofy moments with friends.
items.filming_suggestion: Use a cinematic, trending audio. Edit together short, aesthetic video clips from your trip to show your personality outside the gym.

items.idea: A 5-minute morning mobility routine to do after a long car ride. Great for stiff hips and backs.
items.filming_suggestion: Film on the floor of your Airbnb or hotel room. Speed up the clips slightly and point to the muscle being stretched with text.

items.idea: The 'I just got back from vacation and have no groceries' high-protein meal using only pantry staples.
items.filming_suggestion: Film in your kitchen. Show the 'empty' fridge then pull out pantry items like canned tuna, beans, and rice to create the meal.

items.idea: First day back in the gym after a week off feels weird. Me too. Let's take it easy together.
items.filming_suggestion: Show yourself warming up slowly and lifting lighter than usual. Use a voiceover about listening to your body and easing back in.

meta.case: persona + events -> varied plan; unique sorted day_index within 1..30, pillars rotate, activation week front-loaded
